Prediction: No

Reasoning: The current visit record explicitly details diagnoses related to renal disease (End stage renal disease, Nephritis and nephropathy), metabolic disturbances (Hyperpotassemia, Acidosis), and diabetes (Diabetes with renal manifestations, Polyneuropathy in diabetes). While the patient is on insulin, there is no explicit mention of hypercholesterolemia, hyperlipidemia, or any other specific lipid disorder diagnosis in the current record. The medications prescribed (Insulin, Cinacalcet HCl, Diltiazem Extended-Release) and procedures performed (Hemodialysis) are primarily aimed at managing renal and diabetic complications, not explicitly managing a primary lipid disorder. Therefore, based strictly on the evidence provided, there is no strong and explicit indication that a diagnosis of "Disorders of Lipid Metabolism" will be coded in the next hospital visit.
